message: This is like a face-to-face meeting before the film's crank in.

I always hold this kind of party before the film's crank in. It's a chance to meet everyone and makes the work easier, right?
I think that's a great idea.
Right. There’s the added benefit of easily gauging who gets along and who’s not in good spirits.

I'm not saying that's the intention.

The upcoming movie is set in the major leagues. I plan to have other major leaguers besides Guy appear to capture a more realistic picture.
What’s the story about?
It's about a player on the verge of dropping out who makes a miraculous comeback.
Guy isn’t the main character, right?
No, he’s not. But his role is important to the story.

The upcoming movie is an ambitious piece that seeks to break new ground, so naturally, I'm getting pumped.

As a director, I believe you can create great works in any genre.
